sanskrit: महात्मानस्तु मां पार्थ दैवीं प्रकृतिमाश्रिताः । भजन्त्यनन्यमनसो ज्ञात्वा भूतादिमव्ययम् ॥
hindiTranslation: परन्तु हे पृथानन्दन  दैवी प्रकृतिके आश्रित अनन्यमनवाले महात्मालोग मुझे सम्पूर्ण प्राणियोंका आदि और अविनाशी समझकर मेरा भजन करते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: But, Arjuna, the great-minded ones, relying on the divine nature, worship Me with one-pointed minds, knowing Me as the unborn and imperishable source of all beings.
essence: True devotion begins when the mind recognises the imperishable source.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Noble people rely on the divine nature, stay focused on Krishna alone, and worship him as the unborn, imperishable source of all beings.
simpleInsight: Devotion becomes steady when the mind stops scattering and recognises what never changes.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna stands frozen while Krishna explains why some people reject him and others see clearly. The great-minded ones, unlike the deluded, take refuge in the divine nature and worship Krishna as the beginning and end of all beings.
modernRelevance: You are standing at the edge of a hard choice, and every noisy opinion is pulling you apart. This verse points to the mind that settles, sees what is lasting, and moves from that place.
translationLiteral: महात्मानः (great-minded ones) / तु (but) / माम् (Me) / पार्थ (Arjuna) / दैवीं प्रकृतिम् (divine nature) / आश्रिताः (having taken refuge in) / भजन्ति (worship) / अनन्य-मनसः (with minds fixed on nothing else) / ज्ञात्वा (having known) / भूत-आदिम् (the source of beings) / अव्ययम् (imperishable)
insight: It shifts devotion from emotion alone to clear recognition and single-hearted attention.

quoteVariants.text: Great-hearted ones rest in the divine nature and worship the imperishable source of all beings.
One mind. Divine refuge. Imperishable source.
The great-hearted turn to the imperishable source.
Rest in the divine nature; worship the source.
